Acme was founded in 2019 with a single mission: make enterprise-grade marketing tools accessible to every team, regardless of size.

Our Story

export default function BlogPost({ params }: { params: { slug: string } }) { return (
Engineering

How We Cut API Latency by 60%

Jun 22, 2026 · 8 min read

When our p99 latency crept above 800ms, we knew something had to change. Here's the profiling session, the five fixes, and the result.

The Problem

Our main API endpoint was serving the home feed for every user on every request — re-fetching from the database, re-ranking, re-serializing. At 5k requests per minute this started to show.

What We Found

A 45-minute profiling session with py-spy revealed that 60% of request time was spent in a single ORM call we'd marked as "fast enough" a year ago.

The Fix

We added a two-layer cache: Redis for the ranked feed (TTL 30s) and an in-process LRU for user preferences (TTL 5m). p99 dropped from 800ms to 320ms in the first deploy.

); }
